title: Global Transaction ('G')
text: Global Transaction ('G') Provide Uniface with the DBMS-specific information that the transaction manager (TM) needs to log on to the DBMS. Description Uniface indicates which transaction protocol the TM is using. The connector provides the DBMS-specific information which is needed for the specified protocol. The XA protocol is supported. For more information see the "TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors" topic. From Uniface The DBMS connector must be TM-capable, ( TMCapable ). The current path must be TM-controlled and the connector logon request has not yet been sent. Table 1. Information Available from Uniface Data Structure->Element Description uctrl->umisc Transaction protocol used; ‘XA’ for XA (X/Open DTP Model XA). To Uniface Information that is supplied to Uniface for the Global Transaction request, and the connector request return values. Table 2. Information supplied to Uniface protocol XA: Data Structure->Element Description uctrl->umisc Points to the address of the DBMS’s resource manager switch structure. Each DBMS provides a DBMS-specific switch table of type xa_switch_t . For example, send the address of infx_xa_switch for INFORMIX-ONLINE. When both static and dynamic registration is possible, use static. For example, with Oracle8, static xaosw or dynamic xaoswd can be used. Table 3. Connector Request Return Values Value Description 0 Request was successful. -1 Protocol not supported. -3 Exceptional error. -9 I/O server logon error. -16 DBMS reported network error. Related Links TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors A database connector can be controlled by a transaction manager only if it is TM-capable. Uniface does not provide TM-controlled connectors and does not directly support third-party transaction managers. However, you can use the APIs provided by the Database Connector Interface to build a custom database connector that does. uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table. Transactions Most DBMSs support the concept of transactions. A transaction is a sequence of DML statements which constitute a logical unit of work. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
